commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: If your whole body therefore be full of light, having no part dark, the whole shall be full of light, as when the bright shining of a lamp does give you light. Cornelius a Lapide If thy whole body therefore be full of light. the whole shall be full of light. Not the body, but the whole man and all his faculties and powers. But Toletus gives a different rendering: "If the eye, which is the principal and most noble part of the body, is full of light, then by means of it the whole body will be enlightened.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: The whole shall be lightsome. Not only all thy body, but all about thee; all thy ways and actions. (Witham)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
